name: Mondaire Jones
subject: I stand with the NBA and WNBA players striking for Black lives.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mondaire for Congress_ Our mailing address is:
body:
I wish we saw the same commitment to Black lives from our President. | | | [
](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zdovuQh6AnzseedbOztB5U3N720NDadj7zRuigSklZR7U7bk8iYv7pc-Cp9y3EK8CXP-g5HQxLh_toBU2QT9Fbs8-YcoUMsM7A5S4DkpjJhypwBZm2HrjGVbDKTuv_w1ipKeUz6oVV6McJjE5X42nTR1is_A1FY4Iyod9lP31g3x14VV6NB7RipUkqL700XeZsgjFqkmIprQUFv8k8hdkMDzkNuuhNWRkb6qEKGo3ldvuxDtNnS7IidyX7UQTNWZ1gfWqXDw-IBoWqYGG1mdXVwN-VS6UQYOvAmUEAROf7I3bSSXF2fhTJJjTpEbAunlTQByIM71uUiWRVJEUoTyqGILjyHA1yqqM6FuDF7515-r_iTuc3vFixsCgAO9EsSmTOK9cbR7iXD1HDO06hCWcP2ErIYC09zxR0pOxREadSpw/34p/FhLClZ4xThW6OnRO8CR0Lw/h0/J2t5XXT205UK1RrfUgIdOp_JJTylLL47kMM8Vmbh9Ho) Friends, This week, athletes across the NBA, the WNBA, and other professional sports leagues have been using their platforms to bring attention to pervasive racial injustices in our Black communities. I’m so moved to see so many leaders standing on the side of justice. I wish we saw the same commitment to Black lives from our President and leaders in the Senate. We need bold leadership — from our legislators, our organizers, and our public figures — to fight systemic racism and injustice. As long as Black people continue to be attacked in the streets by the very people who are supposed to be protecting us, I will not stay silent. I commit to using my platform as a member of Congress to keep up this fight. Every single one of us has a platform. No matter whether your reach is a nationwide audience or within your family, we can all commit to using our platforms and privilege for good. **Black Lives Matter, today and every day.** Yours in the Struggle,
Mondaire
**We need radical, structural change to end police violence and racial injustice in this country. Mondaire is ready to fight for this change in Congress, but he can’t get there without your support.** **[Make a donation to our campaign today to support our movement to bring real progressive change to this country.](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zdovuQh6AnzseedbOztB5U3N720NDadj7zRuigSklZR7U7bk8iYv7pc-Cp9y3EK8D4HmMU5GGjOT_qh33OMnyLEA2dTpiExtY7lf6X_rw5Y1a91hkaN5RP434_cwmmWZG1tTcvurwdDuZD9cvJ3wHkoAQju8CVMoFqjDZ4MYY0oaD9rgGW14Et80iFPtB9enfkPZ3Np9aU2W6Efo8cGl_cIFqpQDaEgOilhAWYkmsSGilTSkLy7PgoUEq5fpZNchXs5T-yKvgW8SAFE_C_efr1PDoiaRoX7MWilqGDkyWLv0TLjHvV4SPtqPcMatHXpBEfd-gj4bMVMNJ2hpRlaoifakQZMT8gmoccWu9Q5ucdLYm-1FetMhZIDh-NcLegPfzR471b_0TX5gkr9cWuGD2RJHAxnVGrsAC029k_alqKAQ/34p/FhLClZ4xThW6OnRO8CR0Lw/h1/BnXnnx7xzDK4Vi5Hc_QZc0ED-r-NOIQqffcpcife9qg)** [DONATE](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zdovuQh6AnzseedbOztB5U3N720NDadj7zRuigSklZR7U7bk8iYv7pc-Cp9y3EK8C02tzfjw11abwuaMql9w-DVd3DFmbbnk8Mm7qUaEC5DLlA9pbyTuIbo-8TLU1WqT1rrkA0-bkuXTTT-ydL54lB1nmFnaRDMUQ0IEEXzCeUvpdwNqqG5iCpqcepMyKYDJB7NlsdDfH5Qqmr46FTNkmk0KbJyqlfk_Mps-F3n1l-7GPSBu4Nn0s7VST1SrDhxetjSgoGwH98R0jorY0Dmo5s0o4rIjT7jGL7QW1Ry63YZO86QYAdsUjWxxjYxr3mf9vCIi_6znOteeB-ygFsUpwcGc8laXPzgakkeiZZsnj4fOYoiPmoMJaz1OcJqVO4bAUxQd6j-BXMHNShVace43D7J5Bmg7a2OvASytkIWm4sYg/34p/FhLClZ4xThW6OnRO8CR0Lw/h2/g38OzQIJ4V2d-P5I2IIioX3Rb_90eVCNoMYUxR4D_9A)

name: Biden-Harris 2020
subject: you've been popular this week, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
We are at a critical moment in this election season. |
---

Peter,

We are at a critical moment in this election season. Monday night marks our first public deadline of the general election with your official Democratic nominees Joe Biden and Kamala Harris.

And this August end-of-month fundraising deadline comes at a time when we need your support the most. Last month, Trump outraised us by $25 million, and he's touting fundraising numbers from his convention that could beat ours yet again.

We can't afford to fall short of this first public goal, which is why so many people reached out this month asking for your help.

**Take a look at all the Democrats who took some time to reach out to you directly about our August goal:** | [](https://go.joebiden.%3D%3D/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_aug_eom?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.08.28_B2_everyoneemailed_X__F1_S2_C1__full&amount=100&amounts=50,100,250,500&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.08.28_B2_everyoneemailed_X__F1_S2_C1__full")

We may only have 67 days until November 3, but your donations help us maximize the time we have left, to motivate as many Americans as possible to join our fight for the soul of the nation.

As Vice President Biden wrote to you earlier this week, _" It's on us to do what it takes to put our country on a different path -- a path of hope, love, and light."_

If you're unable to give today, we also want to be clear that's okay. We know that times are difficult right now.

**But if you can spare anything at all before Monday night to help us reach our August end-of-month fundraising goal, we 'd be beyond grateful.**

Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email, with a check, or with a spouse -- thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If we got your name or any information wrong -- we're so sorry! To update and correct your information [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7546/31260570/VEsHAw/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/update-your-information"). If you would like to receive fewer emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7545/31260570/VEsHAg/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-emails"). If you would like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7544/31260570/VEsHDQ/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/content/Unsubscribe").

Peter, thank you so much for supporting Joe Biden and Kamala Harris's Presidential campaign.

[ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7543/31260570/VEsHDA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer&refcode=email-bsd-20190515host-b20_FDR_states_NA-F1_S1_C1")

This isn't the time to be complacent. If you are ready to fight for the soul of this nation, you can start by donating to elect Joe Biden by clicking the button below.

| [DONATE TO ELECT JOE AND KAMALA »](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7542/31260570/VEsEBQ/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_footer?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2019.08.07_B8_footer_X__F1_S1_C1__footer&amount=25")

We know we send a lot of emails, and we are sorry about that. The reason? We are relying on grassroots supporters like you (we're serious!).

But Peter, we don't want to bother you. If you'd like to only receive our most important emails, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7545/31260570/VEsEBA/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/less-emails"). To make a contribution by mail, [click here](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/2931b05d/1cd0ac28/151362c0c/357f7540/31260570/VEsEAQ/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://joebiden.com/contribute-by-mail/") for instructions.

We sincerely thank you for your help and support.
\- The entire Joe Biden for President team

name: Kate Roberts, Powered By People
subject: Did you see Beto’s email?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

Did you see Beto's email, Friend?

We need all hands on deck if we’re going to flip the Texas State House
this election. That’s why we’re teaming up with some of the amazing local
candidates running across Texas for our next round of phone banks. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42547?t=1001&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Can
you join me, Beto, some local candidates, and other Powered by People
volunteers to make calls to mobilize Texas voters?

Upcoming events:
[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43024?source=ema20200828-103033&t=1002&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Virtual Phone Bank with local candidates and Beto
[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43024?source=ema20200828-103033&t=1003&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Monday, August 31, 6:30 pm CT

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43024?source=ema20200828-103033&t=1004&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Get more info or RSVP »

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43025?source=ema20200828-103033&t=1005&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Virtual Phone Bank with local candidates and Beto
[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43025?source=ema20200828-103033&t=1006&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Wednesday, September 2, 6:30 pm CT

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43025?source=ema20200828-103033&t=1007&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Get more info or RSVP »

If you’ve been on any of our previous phone banks, you know we’ve been
calling Texas voters to determine their likelihood of voting for Democrats
in general. Now, we’re calling voters in these candidates’ districts to
make sure their voters know their names and are planning on specifically
voting for Democrats up and down the ballot.

We know it’s possible to flip Texas blue. It’s been awhile since it
happened, but we know it’s possible – but only if we work together.

As Beto said, “You don’t want to wonder on the night of November 3rd, as
the returns begin to roll in, what more you could have done.” [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42547?t=1008&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5 ]Join us
in talking to voters, Friend – you won’t regret it.

See you on the dialer,

Kate Roberts
Powered by People

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1009&akid=103033%2E444211%2EpkFHX5

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

| | Friend, This is the second visit by the President and Vice President in as many weeks. name: Preet Bharara
subject: Donald Trump may have fired me. But my work continues.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | Hi Peter,

My name might sound familiar, but if you can't quite remember why, I'm a former federal prosecutor for the Southern District of New York -- _the one Donald Trump fired in 2017._

Since then, I've spent a lot of time working to elect Democrats up and down the ballot, because I know that the only way we come back from this presidential administration is by defeating Trump and building a strong bench of Democrats in the House and Senate.

This month, my sons challenged me to donate $500 each day to one of their favorite congressional candidates. Christy Smith was on that list, and I donated earlier this month. **I'm proud to do my part to help flip CA-25 back to blue.**

[](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb370/72551406/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in")
**Now I have a challenge for you: Can you help raise another $2,300 to match my donation?** See, I promised Christy that while I couldn't personally contribute the maximum amount ($2,800) to her campaign, I'd find a way to raise the rest to help her win.

**[Peter, will you meet my challenge? Pitch in $5 or whatever you can to help hit that $2,800 I know we can raise together.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb370/72551406/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in")**

Here's the thing: Christy's opponent takes donations from D.C. lobbyists and can reach out to dozens each day for a maximum donation of $2,800. Christy won't do that -- she believes that leaders should be sent to Congress by their constituents, not special interests.

She's proud that hers is a campaign backed by grassroots supporters whose average online donation is **just $22.44.**

If we can prove that we can raise a max-out donation with just a single email, think about how powerful that message is. It's proof that a grassroots campaign can compete with the special interests.

**[If you're able, please pitch in $5 -- or $22.44! -- to Christy's campaign today. Let's raise that $2,800 and send a message about the power of grassroots support.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb370/72551406/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in")** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [Express Donate $5](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb371/72551406/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in&express_lane=true&amount=5") | [Express Donate $22.44](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb372/72551406/VEsD/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in&express_lane=true&amount=22.44")
---|---
[Express Donate $50](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb373/72551406/VEsA/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in&express_lane=true&amount=50") | [Express Donate $100](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb34c/72551406/VEsB/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in&express_lane=true&amount=100")
[Express Donate $250](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb34d/72551406/VEsO/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in&express_lane=true&amount=250") | [Donate Other Amount](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb370/72551406/VEsP/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in")
Thank you. Together, we can hold the House majority.

\-- Preet Bharara

[](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb370/72551406/VEsHBQ/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in")
Christy Smith is a community leader from CA-25 who has fought for her neighbors in Ventura and LA county on the school board and in the California state legislature. Now she needs our help to defeat Mike Garcia and flip this top pick-up House seat blue in November. Help elect a champion for our health care, our schools, and our rights. [DONATE](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb370/72551406/VEsHBA/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA4MjhfRU9NM3ByZWV0X2luIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2008_email_christy_eom?refcode=em20_200828_EOM3preet_in")
---

PO Box 301, 24307 Magic Mountain Pkwy, Valencia, CA 91355.

If you no longer want to hear from Christy Smith, [**click here to get off all our lists instantly.**](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb34f/72551406/VEsHBg/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com") Questions or concerns? [**Contact us here**](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bd8d/29776869/9518294/569eb348/72551406/VEsHAQ/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://www.christyforcongress.org/contact"). |

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: American greatness
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
This week, American greatness was on display. I hope you had the opportunity to tune into the 2020 Republican National Convention | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/american-greatness?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=742ca5c90f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**American Greatness**_
This week, American greatness was on display. I hope you had the opportunity to tune into the 2020 Republican National Convention, because it was a four-night display of the people, principles and values that make the United States the "greatest and most exceptional nation in the history of the world."

I had the honor of attending **President Trump's** speech last night at the White House -- and I have to tell you, being there in person, surrounded by so many symbols of our history, **President Trump** provided the perfect commentary on where we've been as a nation -- and where, together, we can go. "For America, nothing is impossible." - **President Trump**

Under the continued leadership of**President Trump** and **Vice President Pence** , America will continue to be the home of growing jobs, security at home, the sanctity of life, and the protection of the 2nd Amendment.

Over the last four nights, we heard from everyday Americans like you and me -- small business owners, farmers, teachers, local elected officials, and folks from the heartland of America -- who are better today thanks to **President Trump** and are proud to join in support to continue this mission.

Are you feeling inspired? I certainly am. If so, I encourage you -- let's make sure that we Keep America Great, and let's ensure more Republican victories this November. [Sign up to help us reach our fellow Hoosier voters here at home](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c05ed6fdea&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- and most importantly, get ready to vote!

-Chairman Kyle Hupfer

P.S. In addition to the pomp and circumstance in the evenings, the RNC held its official business meetings on Monday in Charlotte, and while there, I was honored to officially nominate **Mike Pence** to be our Republican nominee for Vice President, and to cast all of Indiana's 58 delegates for **President Trump**. Watch the videos [here ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0ce2abc5e1&e=1acd9bb3d6)and [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3d8b062ac1&e=1acd9bb3d6).
---
|
---
| | **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c271ee38cb&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Start Your Engines_** On Sunday, **Governor Holcomb** joined fellow Hoosiers and fans worldwide to watch “The Greatest Spectacle in Racing!"

He started off race day with some media interviews and then [watched the race from home with a group of his best friends](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1f0461d80d&e=1acd9bb3d6). It may have looked a little different this year without 300,000 fans in the stands, but the Indy 500 is a Hoosier tradition that is not contained to the Speedway. Across the state Hoosiers gathered safely to watch the race and spend time with friends and family, and that's what it's all about. Congratulations, Takuma Sato, for winning this year's race and becoming a two-time Indy 500 winner!

To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make sure you're following** Governor Holcomb** on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c70430baa0&e=1acd9bb3d6), [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fc87e5dc2f&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [Instagram](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=80c167a797&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b2456700e9&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_A Different Kind of Campaign_** Earlier this week, **Governor Holcomb's** reelection campaign launched its [second campaign ad](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=76ce56a0a2&e=1acd9bb3d6) of the cycle! The ad titled ["Different Kind of Campaign"](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2676b102dd&e=1acd9bb3d6) notes the strong bonds that Hoosiers have built with each other during the pandemic. **Governor Holcomb** is proud of the resiliency that Hoosiers have shown and is optimistic that -- together -- we can build an even stronger Indiana for future generations. _**“We’ve bonded together through our shared Hoosier values. We are One Indiana, closer than ever before and this election shouldn’t divide us. We’ll run a campaign people can be proud of.** ” **-Governor Holcomb**_ [**\-- > Click here to watch the full ad <\--**](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=10582c8ada&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=61f56e0ac2&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Watch: Victoria Spartz’s New TV Ad**_
**Victoria Spartz** has released a message coming to 5th District airwaves entitled "Bold Thinking."

The Spartz campaign said, _"This commercial highlights the proven leadership and tested experience that will make**Victoria Spartz** the leader Hoosiers in the 5th District will be proud of. A_ _s a finance executive, business owner, and farmer, Victoria will bring her extensive experience to Washington."_
**[\--> Click here to watch the Spartz's new TV ad <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a65e672020&e=1acd9bb3d6)**
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
---
|
---
---
|
---
---
| **_How Did You Watch the RNC?_**
Across the state, the Indiana Republican Party and your local Republican Parties teamed up to host safe watch party events for the Republican National Convention. Check out the photos from just some of these events!
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=098b4f76bc&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Watch: President Trump at the RNC**_
In case you missed it last night, we have **President Donald Trump's** [full acceptance speech](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b5609a1428&e=1acd9bb3d6) available to watch! This is the most important election in our nation's history, and with four more years **President Trump** and **Vice President Pence** will work hard to Keep America Great.
**[\-- > Click here to watch President Trump's full acceptance speech <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=31d3288458&e=1acd9bb3d6)**
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=020249216b&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Watch: Vice President Pence at the RNC_**
Our very own **Vice President Mike Pence** gave his acceptance speech at the Republican National Convention on Wednesday night. During his speech, **VP Pence** discussed the Trump administration's work fighting politics as usual in Washington, building up our economy, and keeping America safe and strong through this pandemic.

**Vice President Pence** said, "In our first three years, we built the greatest economy in the world. We made America great again." [\--> Click here to watch the full acceptance speech <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c2425cb3bf&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
|
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cc901cb0fe&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Election Day is 67 Days Away!_** **Governor Holcomb** and **Secretary of State Connie Lawson** gave an update on election preparations this week -- and the message was clear: This year, it remains safe and secure to vote!
* On November 3, the polls will be open for you to vote in person!
* All Hoosiers have the opportunity to vote early (over 28 days). Skip the lines and vote early starting October 6.
* Absentee voting protocol is in place as usual. Curious if you are eligible? Check [here.](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=776b66979c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* Your vote is protected as measures are in place to secure the voting process.
* Voter registration is open now through October 5. [Check to make sure](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=66a9dbed6f&e=1acd9bb3d6) your registration is up to date.
* Safety is the top priority, as always. PPE materials will be distributed to all poll workers and voters in all counties thanks to the generous help of the Indiana National Guard. Social distancing will also be enforced.

Thank you, **Governor Holcomb** and **Secretary of State Connie Lawson** , for your leadership in keeping our elections safe and secure!
---
|

clean_body: This week, American greatness was on display. I hope you had the opportunity to tune into the 2020 Republican National Convention | | | | | | | | American Greatness This week, American greatness was on display. I hope you had the opportunity to tune into the 2020 Republican National Convention, because it was a four-night display of the people, principles and values that make the United States the "greatest and most exceptional nation in the history of the world." I had the honor of attending President Trump's speech last night at the White House -- and I have to tell you, being there in person, surrounded by so many symbols of our history, President Trump provided the perfect commentary on where we've been as a nation -- and where, together, we can go. "For America, nothing is impossible." - President Trump Under the continued leadership ofPresident Trump and Vice President Pence , America will continue to be the home of growing jobs, security at home, the sanctity of life, and the protection of the 2nd Amendment. Over the last four nights, we heard from everyday Americans like you and me -- small business owners, farmers, teachers, local elected officials, and folks from the heartland of America -- who are better today thanks to President Trump and are proud to join in support to continue this mission. Are you feeling inspired? I certainly am. If so, I encourage you -- let's make sure that we Keep America Great, and let's ensure more Republican victories this November. \-- and most importantly, get ready to vote! -Chairman Kyle Hupfer P.S. In addition to the pomp and circumstance in the evenings, the RNC held its official business meetings on Monday in Charlotte, and while there, I was honored to officially nominate Mike Pence to be our Republican nominee for Vice President, and to cast all of Indiana's 58 delegates for President Trump. Watch the videos . | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Start Your Engines On Sunday, Governor Holcomb joined fellow Hoosiers and fans worldwide to watch “The Greatest Spectacle in Racing!" He started off race day with some media interviews and then . It may have looked a little different this year without 300,000 fans in the stands, but the Indy 500 is a Hoosier tradition that is not contained to the Speedway. Across the state Hoosiers gathered safely to watch the race and spend time with friends and family, and that's what it's all about. Congratulations, Takuma Sato, for winning this year's race and becoming a two-time Indy 500 winner! To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make sure you're following Governor Holcomb on , and ! | | | A Different Kind of Campaign Earlier this week, Governor Holcomb's reelection campaign launched its of the cycle! The ad titled notes the strong bonds that Hoosiers have built with each other during the pandemic. Governor Holcomb is proud of the resiliency that Hoosiers have shown and is optimistic that -- together -- we can build an even stronger Indiana for future generations. “We’ve bonded together through our shared Hoosier values. We are One Indiana, closer than ever before and this election shouldn’t divide us. We’ll run a campaign people can be proud of. ” -Governor Holcomb | | | Watch: Victoria Spartz’s New TV Ad Victoria Spartz has released a message coming to 5th District airwaves entitled "Bold Thinking." The Spartz campaign said, "This commercial highlights the proven leadership and tested experience that will makeVictoria Spartz the leader Hoosiers in the 5th District will be proud of. A s a finance executive, business owner, and farmer, Victoria will bring her extensive experience to Washington." | | | | | | How Did You Watch the RNC? Across the state, the Indiana Republican Party and your local Republican Parties teamed up to host safe watch party events for the Republican National Convention. Check out the photos from just some of these events! | | | Watch: President Trump at the RNC In case you missed it last night, we have President Donald Trump's available to watch! This is the most important election in our nation's history, and with four more years President Trump and Vice President Pence will work hard to Keep America Great. | | | Watch: Vice President Pence at the RNC Our very own Vice President Mike Pence gave his acceptance speech at the Republican National Convention on Wednesday night. During his speech, VP Pence discussed the Trump administration's work fighting politics as usual in Washington, building up our economy, and keeping America safe and strong through this pandemic. Vice President Pence said, "In our first three years, we built the greatest economy in the world. We made America great again." | | | | Election Day is 67 Days Away! Governor Holcomb and Secretary of State Connie Lawson gave an update on election preparations this week -- and the message was clear: This year, it remains safe and secure to vote! On November 3, the polls will be open for you to vote in person! All Hoosiers have the opportunity to vote early (over 28 days). Skip the lines and vote early starting October 6. Absentee voting protocol is in place as usual. Curious if you are eligible? Check Your vote is protected as measures are in place to secure the voting process. Voter registration is open now through October 5. your registration is up to date. Safety is the top priority, as always. PPE materials will be distributed to all poll workers and voters in all counties thanks to the generous help of the Indiana National Guard. Social distancing will also be enforced. Thank you, Governor Holcomb and Secretary of State Connie Lawson , for your leadership in keeping our elections safe and secure! | | Upcoming Events : Patriotic Rally to Honor Servicemen : Perry/Spencer GOP Picnic : Warren County Republican Patriot Day Golf Outing : BBQ with the Blue : Wells County Lincoln Day Dinner & Reagan Rally : 9th Annual Chairman's GOP Rally and Cookout : White County Lincoln Dinner : Brown County Lincoln Day Dinner : Knox County Annual Spot Shoot : Orange County GOP Annual Fall Dinner : Kosciusko County Republican Fall Fish Fry : Lincoln-Reagan Fall Dinner : Adams County Lincoln Day Dinner Have an event to add? Please email it to mailto:info@indiana.gop! | | News You Can Use Governor Eric Holcomb Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch Senator Todd Young Senator Mike Braun Congresswoman Jackie Walorski Congressman Jim Banks Congressman Jim Baird Congresswoman Susan Brooks Congressman Greg Pence Congressman Larry Bucshon Congressman Trey Hollingsworth | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2020 Indiana Republican State Committee, Inc., All rights reserved.

| | Team ― this campaign is gearing up to be one of the closest races in Congress. That means my campaign and I are spending a LOT of time on the little things that can add up to a win in November. But I don’t want to lose sight of the fundamental things that made me get in this race to begin with:
* **Securing healthcare for all,** because every American deserves quality healthcare, regardless of their financial situation.
* **Seeking racial justice and police reform** to demand accountability from those who have broken their sworn oath to protect and serve their communities.
* **Addressing the existential threat of climate change** to secure a healthier future for our children and millions of high-paying green jobs secured through a Green New Deal.
* **Protecting the right to vote** as one of our most fundamental and crucial rights granted to all people.

All of these policies help us to achieve a more just and fair society. I’ll never stop fighting for a future we can all be proud to leave for our kids. [**If you’re in this fight with me, please chip into my campaign's $50,000 End-of-Month goal to help bring our message to the voters of TX-10.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18900006/243133881/1255145819?refcode=SGL_EM_FR_200828_EOM9_U1_X1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOWE5OWMxNS03ZWU5LWVhMTEtOGIwMy0wMDE1NWQwMzk0YmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vfc2Hauf6VDcusfH60fD5iJG56PEgw8tn335veyNo70=) | | | [Chip in $25 (x3!) →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18900007/243133882/1255145819?refcode=SGL_EM_FR_200828_EOM9_U1_X1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiOWE5OWMxNS03ZWU5LWVhMTEtOGIwMy0wMDE1NWQwMzk0YmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=vfc2Hauf6VDcusfH60fD5iJG56PEgw8tn335veyNo70=)

clean_body: | = | | Team ― this campaign is gearing up to be one of the closest races in Congress. That means my campaign and I are spending a LOT of time on the little things that can add up to a win in November. But I don’t want to lose sight of the fundamental things that made me get in this race to begin with: Securing healthcare for all, because every American deserves quality healthcare, regardless of their financial situation. Seeking racial justice and police reform to demand accountability from those who have broken their sworn oath to protect and serve their communities. Addressing the existential threat of climate change to secure a healthier future for our children and millions of high-paying green jobs secured through a Green New Deal. Protecting the right to vote as one of our most fundamental and crucial rights granted to all people. All of these policies help us to achieve a more just and fair society. I’ll never stop fighting for a future we can all be proud to leave for our kids. = | | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = These goals are crucial to keeping our campaign running competitively. Our opponent and the Washington Republicans behind him have dumped MILLIONS to drown out our progressive message. They’re spending all this money because they know they can’t compete on a level playing field, friends. We know we’ve got the winning ideas ― but we’re still only 62% of the way to our End-of-Month goal, and we won’t be able to compete if we don’t get there.= | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = | | = Thanks for your support, friends. ❌ She continues to remain SILENT on the issues that matter to our nation right now, ❌ She sides with Far-Left radical in wanting to DEFUND our police force, ❌ And she passively AGREES with Nancy Pelosi when she calls our President and Commander in Chief, Donald J. Trump a DOMESTIC terrorist.
This absolutely baffles me.
The fact that our nation has had a terrorist attack nearly 20 years ago and the Speaker of the House can say that President Trump is a larger threat than that accompanying part is blasphemous. And Peter, you may wonder where our current congresswoman sits: Silent and idle, refusing to speak out against her party. name: Wesley Hunt for Congress
subject: Fletcher Bill Puts Houston Disaster Response Equipment on The Chopping Block
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
# **Fletcher Bill Puts Houston Disaster Response Equipment on The Chopping Block**
_Acevedo says bill puts “the safety of the American people at risk”_
Houston, TX – This week, just before Houston luckily dodged the destructive winds of Hurricane Laura, Texas Business Daily reported that a bill supported by 7th District Congresswoman Lizzie Fletcher would have [“huge consequences for Houston's resources for disaster response.”](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=4b57453e2f9144cbbf3f9cc48f61a7fd&_e=o35VX2S6Aw6r0KVyDttDKeG4-unis8y06jQqaya3rndc04Bka4JlddVtN6tgIhva_lJHkEHo2aaEIQhZbNIwPJUgBjjix9mTuEAfs66oXJsIT1SoOeEWb7zdpMXqlMF9P5m6JAgPoB8GQLE9zVWGadzPrsodaihJIS60vg-tHcC44urB6RMbTfqr4j6SbFdpxChK8Qsh8LXl-XhGSnJVLpveVETfFinJ26Y-F4xF2ubeoz8Zpk8VoC6H_drQKxtISIAWJFIKvzuol6jZG-AJ9uRASmF3RkIOnShEwMGWpu2vsQKFzDkngRTsLEqE9-x4CHWHVWzGsz3kSBiLHDuKg9LGEhM5AFjRrQ48aafT4nQvDTBBwsdkR3OnHaLqtbeIeOSXv5-gTXZx5tjj18bPGg%3D%3D)

H.R. 7120, already passed through the House with support from Fletcher, would restrict the ability of Houston's police departments to access retired military vehicles used in high water rescues. In the wake of Hurricane Harvey’s destruction, these vehicles are credited with rescuing more than [10,000 Houstonians](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=4b57453e2f9144cbbf3f9cc48f61a7fd&_e=o35VX2S6Aw6r0KVyDttDKYE3XH5Nn6PjSlJYDlSzsd-qfGUfV2gEZIHUCi8yfQBV_hjkuhGsBOrc2hcTPU97bwrDENmycAEBBREzWkGoN7QRflJn5p3OQiK66gFmMrXTYxv3Ry03UeVZxQmO-9Pifapznrw_v6-vxV134V4Z7pSJeAheMpInkAABbhJK6JGz-FtEohtokb79Eq2iMaP8_Pwfa-OTBjqIyI48LJ7eSBu_vqbqrPBGdPNnU7IvDlLvAzScuNTh-QUovzCtPH71POXhbNCnh66JJJrUhRI-64qFLbGLTDvBEjuv3D3esOaYTZvjYwQ68zCrItAkVtUV55EqbbeW8CyIzNtt-DkEYkCnDwjhKSgRlt26UjGjLeMSiBwvglhbhh-xjnkEwwod1w%3D%3D).

Houston’s first responders, including Police Chief Art Acevedo raised alarm on the bill, saying, “elected officials, at times, make decisions based on emotion, not on facts, and the operational requirements of police departments. We're just getting ready right now to face Hurricane Laura on the three-year anniversary of Hurricane Harvey. When we limit that type of equipment, we are putting the safety of the American people at risk.”

Fletcher not only voted in favor of the bill in the House, but in fact said that she was [“proud to cosponsor this bill.”](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=4b57453e2f9144cbbf3f9cc48f61a7fd&_e=o35VX2S6Aw6r0KVyDttDKcM2uJB49n52lj2jZhVUhkFK9o_DR29iR9YvM_wsNTJBHUaH5GwvTC9Oa4DTw6MENhhJzBw7YKTcLgnJeSppFCFmCDW9MM96dBz29FVW_4Y6VpcmZ9F8mjhDbT3hXNbXhwYNdIn5SfBMrJazF4-ldXjMT-aS7ybppp9t3bNaMJYPqQ91iuKEry4GfV7hV1HG8gm6ueCfYH_3EeH8QjepM3Jhlsbky5PMRVgde4oZzJLuqwmM_Uu7pKd1coY8cMbSGd2Hi9VYfP6US-EoqPAlY09GG7wH4sMn0GKlFnJ3G_HY)

In response to Lizzie Fletcher's reckless legislation, Wesley Hunt's Campaign Manager Jim Hilk released the following statement.

“The first job of any House Member is to represent their individual district. Lizzie Fletcher didn’t just fail to advocate for her district, but fundamentally undermined the tools available to the first responders who risk their lives to save others during natural disasters. Either Fletcher didn’t actually read the bill that she put her name on as a cosponsor, a thoroughly disqualifying and incompetent act, or she chose to put partisan politics above the health and safety of her constituents. 7th District voters deserve to know why Congresswoman Fletcher chose to cosponsor a bill so destructive to the people of Houston and all of Texas.”
###

****[CONTRIBUTE](http://links.wesleyfortexas.com/u/click?_t=fd5d6e7aa0334e7ebb096a39dc496016&_m=4b57453e2f9144cbbf3f9cc48f61a7fd&_e=o35VX2S6Aw6r0KVyDttDKYkcet4Gv22S19HtdFBxnNIGvDtnj2OU8_wkUXTHXF-5N58MUupf6gKR0x2CZ85lUofS7Q9RZs7Ivt2OqmYvEp7Scs_M9Zf1aQicAEgmy83idUdMq1tNYkZSIxM-69hbIWjg90NHct6LFQNFNaE-qWmxFwChgUdscV4Ze-GGuUaerOLC_BqWoccHQebZkkdyXM00v0uCch2uQNYko4KuJhKkp3Lg9HDjPE-_d7LsmTZKPCXzW13Rv0ngp5xk-b2SxXzM949xQxSipwnacAHPJi4%3D)****

name: Jon Ossoff (personal)
subject: The fight for voting rights continues
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JON OSSOFF FOR SENATE
body:
Remembering the March on Washington 57 years ago |
---
Hi friend,

On August 28, 1963, hundreds of thousands of Americans participated in the March on Washington. It remains one of the largest rallies for human rights in U.S. history.

Led by a team of heroic civil rights heroes, including Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and my mentor, Congressman John Lewis, the March on Washington was a call to action for this country -- and the world -- advocating for civil, economic, and voting rights for Black Americans. It helped pave the way for the Voting Rights Act of 1965.

**57 years later, there are still elected officials in America working to make it harder for citizens to vote, especially here in Georgia.**

Our state is on the frontline of fighting against voter suppression -- which is why _protecting the right to vote is one of my top priorities_.

Over the past year, hundreds of thousands of voters have been purged from Georgia's voter rolls, and tens of thousands more are on the chopping block. What's happened in Georgia over the last decade may be one of the largest mass voter purges in American history.

_My opponent, David Perdue, refuses to stand up to this voter disenfranchisement -- maybe because he thinks it improves his re-election prospects. If Georgians have a harder time voting, he gets to keep the fancy office and continue entertaining donors on the private island where he lives._

**We already know this race is going to be close, and that means it could come down to a few votes.** That's why we're doing EVERYTHING we can to protect voting rights in Georgia.

With your help, we can stop the disenfranchisement of minority and working-class voters and win this race. And then, I will do everything I can in the Senate to protect voting rights -- including fighting for a new Voting Rights Act in honor of John Lewis.

**[So today, on this important anniversary, I'm asking: Can you contribute $10 to our Voter Protection Fund in honor of the March on Washington?](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02821/2501627515/VEsH/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash&amount=10")** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| | [EXPRESS DONATE: $10](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02820/2501627515/VEsE/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash_ex_10")
---
| [EXPRESS DONATE: $20](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02829/2501627515/VEsF/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?express_lane=true&amount=20&refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash_ex_20")
---
| [EXPRESS DONATE: $50](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02830/2501627515/VEsC/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash_ex_50")
---
| [EXPRESS DONATE: $100](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02837/2501627515/VEsD/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash_ex_100")
---
| [Or donate another amount](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f0283b/2501627515/VEsA/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?express_lane_other=true&refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash_ex_other")
---
This election is about much more than me and David Perdue.

It's about standing up for our right to self-govern.

It's about honoring the legacy of John Lewis with a new Voting Rights Act.

It's about fighting corrupt politicians who abuse their power.

**Friend, this can't wait. With the election just over two months away, we need to do everything we can to defy voter suppression TODAY. There's simply too much at stake to do otherwise.**

**[So please, chip in $10 right now to our Voter Protection Fund to help me take this fight against voter suppression all the way to Washington.](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02821/2501627515/VEsB/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash&amount=10")**
| [DONATE NOW](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e24b0/5b2c1286/3ff21989/25f02821/2501627515/VEsO/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_2020_08_28_march_wash?refcode=em_2020_08_28_march_wash&amount=10")
---
With gratitude and courage,

Jon Ossoff

clean_body: Remembering the March on Washington 57 years ago | Hi friend, On August 28, 1963, hundreds of thousands of Americans participated in the March on Washington. It remains one of the largest rallies for human rights in U.S. history. Led by a team of heroic civil rights heroes, including Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and my mentor, Congressman John Lewis, the March on Washington was a call to action for this country -- and the world -- advocating for civil, economic, and voting rights for Black Americans. It helped pave the way for the Voting Rights Act of 1965. 57 years later, there are still elected officials in America working to make it harder for citizens to vote, especially here in Georgia. Our state is on the frontline of fighting against voter suppression -- which is why protecting the right to vote is one of my top priorities. Over the past year, hundreds of thousands of voters have been purged from Georgia's voter rolls, and tens of thousands more are on the chopping block. What's happened in Georgia over the last decade may be one of the largest mass voter purges in American history. My opponent, David Perdue, refuses to stand up to this voter disenfranchisement -- maybe because he thinks it improves his re-election prospects. If Georgians have a harder time voting, he gets to keep the fancy office and continue entertaining donors on the private island where he lives. We already know this race is going to be close, and that means it could come down to a few votes. That's why we're doing EVERYTHING we can to protect voting rights in Georgia. With your help, we can stop the disenfranchisement of minority and working-class voters and win this race. And then, I will do everything I can in the Senate to protect voting rights -- including fighting for a new Voting Rights Act in honor of John Lewis. "" If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" This election is about much more than me and David Perdue. It's about standing up for our right to self-govern. It's about honoring the legacy of John Lewis with a new Voting Rights Act. It's about fighting corrupt politicians who abuse their power. Friend, this can't wait. With the election just over two months away, we need to do everything we can to defy voter suppression TODAY. There's simply too much at stake to do otherwise. "" | "" With gratitude and courage, Jon Ossoff | This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com "" | "" | P.O. Box 450326, Atlanta, GA 31145 PAID FOR BY JON OSSOFF FOR SENATE

America's cities are on fire as rioters loot and pillage them every night in the name of "social justice"--Minneapolis, Seattle, Portland, New York, and now Kenosha, Wisconsin.

****

After President Trump's closing RNC speech at the White House, a mob of rioters assaulted police officers and threatened Trump supporters. Rioters even brought a guillotine, which they used to ritually _behead_ Trump in effigy.

[**Will you pitch in to help restore law and order?**](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDMzMjQyMjkS1&kn=6&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTgyMjI4OTE4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

If you have donated with a different email, with a check, or with a spouse -- thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.

Sri is a fighter for working families whose own deeply personal experience with our broken health care system has guided his fight for systemic change. After his father was diagnosed with cancer, Sri dropped out of school to care for him -- and the bills from his care nearly bankrupted his family. Sri knows that this is a story shared by millions of Americans, and **he’s running to make sure it never happens again.**

After returning to school, graduating, and receiving his Master’s degree, Sri went on to become a diplomat, promoting American values all around the world, including places like Russia, China and Iran.

**Sri knows firsthand what America’s global standing means for the future of democracies around the world, and he can help restore our global standing and ensure America leads on diplomacy once again.**

Now, he’s running for Congress in the midst of a pandemic that has decimated jobs around the country and left families all across Texas struggling. More than ever, Texas families need leaders who are ready to champion their cause, fight for an economic recovery that puts them first and ensure that our government finally works for the people.

Sri is that leader, friend. His story reflects the best of American possibility.

subject: Are YOU in, Peter?

body:
Last night we watched as our President, Donald J. Trump accepted the Republican nomination to be our candidate for November’s election............... Peter, Last night we watched as our President, Donald J. Trump accepted the Republican nomination to be our candidate for November’s election.
Peter, this election will be about two very different visions for America.
The Radical Left is pushing for higher taxes, more regulations, open borders, defunded police departments, and ending fossil fuels.
And us, Republicans led by President Trump, pushing to safely restart our economy, create real jobs, help small businesses, and defend life and liberty.
But Peter, as President Trump accepted his nomination, he also accepted what will be a massive challenge.
He will be ruthlessly attacked over the next few months, but with patriots like you by his side, he will weather the storm and continue to fight for our values.
Today, on the heels of his acceptance speech, he needs to know that you’re with him... that you’ll stand up and fight with him against the Radical Left. **[Will you add your name to show him just clean_body: Last night we watched as our President, Donald J. Trump accepted the Republican nomination to be our candidate for November’s election............... Peter, Last night we watched as our President, Donald J. Trump accepted the Republican nomination to be our candidate for November’s election. Peter, this election will be about two very different visions for America. The Radical Left is pushing for higher taxes, more regulations, open borders, defunded police departments, and ending fossil fuels. And us, Republicans led by President Trump, pushing to safely restart our economy, create real jobs, help small businesses, and defend life and liberty. But Peter, as President Trump accepted his nomination, he also accepted what will be a massive challenge. He will be ruthlessly attacked over the next few months, but with patriots like you by his side, he will weather the storm and continue to fight for our values. Today, on the heels of his acceptance speech, he needs to know that you’re with him... that you’ll stand up and fight with him against the Radical Left. It’s going to take a real collaborative effort among conservatives to defend truth, protect our freedoms, and STOP the Radical-Left, Peter. Rob Portman Paid for by Rob Portman for Senate

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
You Can’t Handle Rioting Radicals Like University Unrest, DOJ Asks for COVID Data on Nursing Home Deaths, Trump Plan Would Cure School Opening Chaos | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4e886aa9c2&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**You Can****’****t Handle Rioting Radicals Like University Unrest**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fd1739e7d8&e=3ee4985eea)
-_New York Post_
“Since the 1960s, campus leaders have perfected a script. Student activists can take over administrative offices or the library. These violations are tolerated, as long as not too much is disrupted . . . In recent months, we have seen this script applied to city streets, where the costs are higher than having to bite your lip as inflamed 19-year-olds berate you,” R.R. Reno writes.
---
|
---
| [**Justice Department Asks New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania for COVID Data on Nursing Home Deaths**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f4e27cb4ee&e=3ee4985eea)
_-Just the News_
“The Justice Department on Wednesday requested COVID-19 data from four states it says required nursing homes to accept residents infected with the coronavirus, policies that may have rendered elderly Americans ‘unnecessarily put at risk’ . . . [and] ‘which may have resulted in the deaths of thousands of elderly nursing home residents,’” Daniel Payne reports.

**MORE:[President Trump takes action to further protect nursing homes](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c3ed253513&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
| [**Trump Plan Would Cure School Re-Opening Chaos**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6736418e26&e=3ee4985eea)
-_RealClearPolitics_
“From Los Angeles to New York, and many stops in between, America’s public school teachers’ unions have demanded that schools remain physically closed, that police forces be defunded, and that unrealistic COVID-testing policies be implemented . . . These unions do not seem to care that it is the most disadvantaged students in our nation who will be most negatively affected by their decisions,” Kevin Nicholson writes.
---
|
---
| [**Enough Is Enough: Trump Gave Veterans Real and Permanent Choice**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d8cb5eb757&e=3ee4985eea)
_-The Hill_
“It’s been more than a year since President Trump ushered in the greatest improvement to veteran health care since World War II, and the media still refuse to credit him with this accomplishment. Reporters are playing word games instead of reporting the facts, and are failing to tell the public what millions of veterans know: the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) has never worked better,” VA Secretary Robert Wilkie writes.
---
|
---
| [**White House Announces $750M Deal for Abbott Labs' COVID-19 Rapid Test**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b13215fcb6&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox Business
“The White House has announced a $750 million deal with Abbott Laboratories Thursday to buy 150 million of its new rapid coronavirus tests,” Lucas Manfredi writes. This major development in testing will save lives by further protecting America’s most vulnerable and will allow our country to get more Americans safely back to work and school.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4594e17729&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1488a244ea&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=471fd023c0)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

Friend,
The Democrats are losing it.

Unlike their dark and depressing anti-Trump Convention, we just put on the most HISTORIC and Patriotic 2020 GOP Convention of all time.

They know their loser candidates, Slow Joe and Phony Kamala, don’t stand a chance against Vice President Pence and me in November. The American People have made it clear that they want FOUR MORE YEARS of my critical America First agenda, and the Left just can’t handle it.

I want to keep our unmatched Convention momentum going. Let’s do something SO HUGE and SO INCREDIBLE that they won’t be able to ignore how strong our movement is.

Our August End-of-Month fundraising deadline is coming up and I need YOUR help to raise another $30,000,000. To help us CRUSH our goal, I’ve activated a 600%-MATCH until 11:59 PM TONIGHT.

I want to know who stood with me when it mattered most, so I’ve asked my team to send me a list of EVERY AMERICAN PATRIOT who donates to this email.

name: Lisa Scheller
subject: Lisa Scheller's Scholarships
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Scheller for Congress, Inc
body:
Lisa Scheller funded and helped to establish the “Roberta and Ernest Scheller Jr. Family Foundation Scholarship” This scholarship is for graduates of Tamaqua High School who wish to pursue a four year college degree at the Pennsylvania state school of their choice. There is also a student center at Lehigh Carbon Community College located in Tamaqua named after Lisa Scheller because of Lisa’s dedication to furthering the youth of this region's education. This wonderful building allows students to gather, study, and relax in between classes. With generous funding, the student center at LCCC houses a bookstore, canteen, business center, and veterans lounge. Several years after Lisa completed a certificate program in computer programming from Pennco Tech, she obtained a B.A. in Math from the University of Colorado and ten years later an M.S. in Manufacturing Systems Engineering. Lisa wants to make sure that every child has the opportunities that she did to achieve the American dream. The important thing to note about Lisa's scholarships, is that they are geared towards those who can not afford to go to school at any cost. Lisa recently endowed a scholarship for high school students in need that enables them to graduate with an associates degree for free. She states “Education is the key that unlocks the door of opportunity.” She understands that the student loan debt and the cost of college is out of control. The Federal government caused this problem by taking over the student loan industry and by driving up the costs of tuition. Lisa believes that our students shouldn’t graduate with crippling student debt. Schools should be accountable. If their students are unable to pay back the loans, the school has some skin in the game. What Lisa does not support is the socialists plans to forgive all student loan debt. That means taking millions of dollars from Eastern Pennsylvanians who paid off their student loans or maybe didn’t go to college and giving that money to someone else. That’s not fair. This scholarship is to push deserving kids to further their education and live their version of the American Dream. [DONATE](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=048d540085e04e3498ffe330dafdbd86&_e=boq71bBdUtFG7B-oLU1OziBnT_u8idYZb7TeBbpdqROyrpuYuA7VIUXU-vOMXTvMuvE_0KYt5Yewgod8FwV9ZXsZfAT0Lj-ZwoHG79-UgMk856948d_ChiGVeRZc68lB2jHfIgc8vaNnAcFb3iqdh1hFamgEQfqLcbeLVnSNnIdmarVL05GB4uwyojbI6fHM_lGJ_iGVf0WcFVqfd-9_tTcpmM7phCFk9uhMt4xwtB04OVINCfpzu0k7J9wNvCDjlq5nsNQ1z9qg41BfIAAuk66DYVzrnJbn0aZ56OF6to8cmwWbj0-zib8FrPMiJb-l0lXOogBNgitz9MCjmh3BNPnfPvvP2Q_T5-fswGCLTetdahiNLb9UtfIQoIcsO9e-f53-PHgahCPibUeyqc4YYWkACyl7aYIw4JYYx-FXEJTPtU9qKHXRUbYhSTneSNEY) Paid for by Lisa Scheller for Congress, Inc. name: Kathy Manning
subject: Let's end this week on a high note
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
This is why Trump and Republicans are so focused on North Carolina ->
|
---

| | All I can say is this: What a week. Postmaster General Louis DeJoy -- an NC-06 resident and a Trump mega-donor -- testified before the House Oversight Committee about operational changes at the USPS that are slowing down mail. The Republican National Convention was also held here in North Carolina, where Republicans nominated Trump for re-election. We've heard it all this week: DeJoy's staunch defense of dangerous USPS changes and President Trump's lies about voter fraud, our election, and our economy. Trump even attacked our governor for prioritizing the North Carolinians' health over the RNC. We need to out-organize Republicans in these final 10 weeks before Election Day. **It couldn 't be any more apparent: We need new leadership in Washington, _and we need it now._** [_**Trump and Republicans are so focused on North Carolina because they know they can 't win in 2020 without it. We need to put in the work right now to make sure they don't succeed this November. name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Weekend Recap: EdOC, FAQs, and immunizations
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
There's a lot going on these days, and it's easy to miss things. That's why I wanted to put together a quick recap for you of the new announcements from the past week.
If you know someone who wants to stay up-to-date on the latest measures Rhode Island is taking to stop the spread of COVID-19, send them this link: [lp.constantcontactpages.com/su/eYMG9tf](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014yQXTrsHGd33fde6bb-rqq16KLsSx2uXIT4To0g89Q7i5Oc7WflGnnscTi-G6yMHsQ7CeeJzaPcSKnhQdpkcqkpnXWzD0GuTayTsb1lzmAgVt3NTpqrpitoHnSTF44e_zOPwq_g6FLmGm90HmkeRl0urcWyxvwN8_PWyqf68gv7wPP98VAIQS8CgWY8thduFOL5J6q3Sia9h9knZt3GOnjAw-io_txkfi3g_dvu7bC5qLGDSwgfaFt-6-v2u-yua_QiKHcmTSs7PH7etymqEF3bU6ssZ6OqcGAfBiSinW3g4Vp0VVnZ1cmm3L9fB2-1dwHB7xV1p3N4=&c=DaWEvjxghR92SxY61fMtg5UTdJatAQkX4G8S3583nTYUhaR-fd8C3w==&ch=Qd1zRMshkdF5XAg2xmgHGb2H4ByjGDSoP-rRubFn3fXd8NyE_Kh4UQ==). The better we all stay informed, the better we'll be able to keep ourselves, our families, and our friends safe from COVID-19.
---
| | |
---|---
|
EDUCATION OPERATIONS CENTER
This week, I was excited to announce that we're launching an Education Operations Center (EdOC). Just like the emergency operations centers we set up during major statewide events, this EdOC will be staffed by people across state agencies - RIDE, RIDOH, DOT, RIPTA, EMA, and others - and will be entirely focused on providing real-time support to our schools, drawing on the operational expertise of the Rhode Island National Guard.

This operations center will be ready and able to assist with anything our schools need when they reopen. This team will support districts to ensure they have the supplies they need, like hand sanitizer or masks. If they need logistical support to ensure strong in-person learning, this team will be able to provide assistance. When they have a student or staff member who tests positive, this team will be on the ground to test, isolate and quarantine, preventing a large outbreak just as we have done with child care and congregate care settings over the past few months.
| | |
---|---
|
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Over the last few weeks, we've received a lot of great questions from families about how we are preparing to send our kids back to school safely this fall. So this week, RIDE Commissioner Angelica Infante-Green, RIDOH Director Dr. Nicole Alexander-Scott, and RIDOH Medical Director Dr. Jim McDonald held a Facebook Live forum to answer some of the most frequently asked questions that families have about school reopening. You can watch [here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0014yQXTrsHGd33fde6bb-rqq16KLsSx2uXIT4To0g89Q7i5Oc7WflGnnXciJhr_hmaqgmohaGqUVxdLsc0jwteQYlOT1yX4yoL8QoebRS5TCDLIWOPPJLIWWdAdPh4N9a4cqSv6NvQA80TFRGvIcIjjSwTreoK5h5laWx_m-5_mS2B9U6tIF0xupSszpotfAZRhknrWR0Ffuk=&c=DaWEvjxghR92SxY61fMtg5UTdJatAQkX4G8S3583nTYUhaR-fd8C3w==&ch=Qd1zRMshkdF5XAg2xmgHGb2H4ByjGDSoP-rRubFn3fXd8NyE_Kh4UQ==).

I'll be back next week with Commissioner Infante-Green, and we'll be joined by Rhode Island Health and Human Services Secretary Womazetta Jones and Providence Superintendent Harrison Peters. We'll be discussing equity in schools, and we want you to be a part of the conversation. If you have questions you think the Secretary or Superintendent could help answer, please reply to this email with them.
| | |
---|---
|
IMMUNIZATIONS

The closures of doctor's offices this spring, coupled with changing routines and closures of immunization clinics in schools, led to a nearly 50% drop in immunizations in March, April and May compared to the previous year. Working with our pediatricians, we launched an aggressive campaign to turn that trend around, and by June we were only 8% below the previous year - a dramatic improvement, but there's still more work to do.

While we made up a lot of lost ground, the reality is that only 75% of kids entering Kindergarten this year have all their necessary immunizations, and it's even lower -- about 50% -- for kids entering 7th grade. As we make plans to get our kids back to school safely this fall, please make sure that your child has the immunizations they need. If they don't, please call your pediatrician to schedule your child's appointment. 1-855-840-4774 to learn more about coverage.
| I hope you find this recap helpful. It's so important that everyone stay engaged and informed so that we can all stay safe and healthy. I'll be back on TV with more updates Monday at 1 p.m.
We're all in this together.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19. clean_body: Your RI COVID-19 News Update | | | | | | | Hello friend, There's a lot going on these days, and it's easy to miss things. That's why I wanted to put together a quick recap for you of the new announcements from the past week. If you know someone who wants to stay up-to-date on the latest measures Rhode Island is taking to stop the spread of COVID-19, send them this link: ==. The better we all stay informed, the better we'll be able to keep ourselves, our families, and our friends safe from COVID-19. | | | ---|--- | EDUCATION OPERATIONS CENTER This week, I was excited to announce that we're launching an Education Operations Center (EdOC). Just like the emergency operations centers we set up during major statewide events, this EdOC will be staffed by people across state agencies - RIDE, RIDOH, DOT, RIPTA, EMA, and others - and will be entirely focused on providing real-time support to our schools, drawing on the operational expertise of the Rhode Island National Guard. This operations center will be ready and able to assist with anything our schools need when they reopen. This team will support districts to ensure they have the supplies they need, like hand sanitizer or masks. If they need logistical support to ensure strong in-person learning, this team will be able to provide assistance. When they have a student or staff member who tests positive, this team will be on the ground to test, isolate and quarantine, preventing a large outbreak just as we have done with child care and congregate care settings over the past few months. | | | ---|--- | FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS Over the last few weeks, we've received a lot of great questions from families about how we are preparing to send our kids back to school safely this fall. So this week, RIDE Commissioner Angelica Infante-Green, RIDOH Director Dr. Nicole Alexander-Scott, and RIDOH Medical Director Dr. Jim McDonald held a Facebook Live forum to answer some of the most frequently asked questions that families have about school reopening. You can watch ==. I'll be back next week with Commissioner Infante-Green, and we'll be joined by Rhode Island Health and Human Services Secretary Womazetta Jones and Providence Superintendent Harrison Peters. We'll be discussing equity in schools, and we want you to be a part of the conversation. If you have questions you think the Secretary or Superintendent could help answer, please reply to this email with them. | | | ---|--- | IMMUNIZATIONS The closures of doctor's offices this spring, coupled with changing routines and closures of immunization clinics in schools, led to a nearly 50% drop in immunizations in March, April and May compared to the previous year. Working with our pediatricians, we launched an aggressive campaign to turn that trend around, and by June we were only 8% below the previous year - a dramatic improvement, but there's still more work to do. While we made up a lot of lost ground, the reality is that only 75% of kids entering Kindergarten this year have all their necessary immunizations, and it's even lower -- about 50% -- for kids entering 7th grade. As we make plans to get our kids back to school safely this fall, please make sure that your child has the immunizations they need. If they don't, please call your pediatrician to schedule your child's appointment. 1-855-840-4774 to learn more about coverage. | I hope you find this recap helpful. It's so important that everyone stay engaged and informed so that we can all stay safe and healthy. I'll be back on TV with more updates Monday at 1 p.m. We're all in this together. -Gina Raimondo Governor P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19. name: Catherine Engelbrecht
subject: WEEKLY UPDATE: Keep Your Eyes on the Prize
body:
| | | | |
---
| | [](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1402)
---
| |
---
|
---
| | WHAT HAPPENED THIS WEEK

For those of you who follow the news, you have probably noticed an influx of headlines from your state, county, and even beyond on vast expansions of voting methods ahead of Election Day. While these developments may seem innocent or even beneficial, it is important that everyone understand the many risks and insufficiencies that come with such expansions. Whether it's proposals for 24-hour voting centers or opening large arenas that can supposedly handle large crowds, voters should not be fooled: these expansions don't address the very basic needs that we have for increased in-person polling and adequate election security. Rather than focus resources we don't have on managing giant voting centers in centralized locations, what we need are more polling places spread out among communities that provide easier access to voters close to where they live and that can handle the influx of people coming to vote.

Regardless of such empty efforts that won't meaningfully address our voting challenges, I urge each and every one of you to stay focused on what matters: registering to vote and showing up at the polls to cast your vote. If you can't vote in person, apply to vote absentee. If your state is only allowing mail ballots, submit your ballot in person if permitted. If you can only vote by mail and have no in-person options, then vote by mail and be sure you closely follow all instructions to ensure your vote is submitted on time and counted. Keep your eyes on the prize. There are many things to worry about during these final days of a presidential election year, but don't allow that to distract you from the most important thing: making your voice heard and casting your vote. Get your friends, families, and neighbors involved. Voting is one of the very most important things that we can do as American citizens to influence the direction and the future of our nation.

Onward,
Catherine Engelbrecht

Episode 14: Mass Mail Mayhem: 2020 Election Goes Postal [WATCH NOW > ](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1408)
Episode 13: 5 Easy Ways to Steal an Election and How You Can Stop It [WATCH NOW >](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1409)
Episode 12: November is Coming... [WATCH NOW >](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1410)
Episode 11: The Truth About Soros, BLM, and Elections [WATCH NOW > ](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1411)
Episode 10: TX Attorney General Ken Paxton [WATCH NOW > ](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1412)
To see all previous episodes, please visit [here](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1403).

---
| | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | IN THE NEWS

The Phony Post Office War. For those who have been following the latest saga over the Post Office, the Wall Street Journal's recent editorial shines a light on how the mainstream media obsession over the issue is in fact just an excuse being made by Democrats for their party's own failure over expanded mail-in voting. Read more: [Wall Street Journal](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1415), 8/23/2020
"...General Louis DeJoy testified last week that the post office has enough money to deliver mail-in ballots, and his operation can’t possibly spend $25 billion that quickly in any case. Democrats voted for so much money as a bailout for the letter-carriers union that endorsed Joe Biden this month. They want the money with no reform strings attached, though the Postal Service has [lost $78 billion since 2007](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1416)... [DeJoy] also said the post office will prioritize ballots over other kinds of first-class mail. This should put the onus on state and local officials to establish adequate rules and deadlines for mail ballots. States like Michigan and New York have deadlines that [are simply too late](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1417) to process the ballots without delays and controversy. That’s the real Nov. 3 scandal to worry about."

Debunking 10 Myths About the US Postal Service. Sure, the Postal Service has its issues, and all voters who can vote in person should do so. But the claims made by Democrat politicians in their efforts to cast blame on USPS for their own vote by mail failures are blatantly false. The Daily Signal takes a deep dive into the truth. Read more: [The Daily Signal](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1418), 8/18/20

**Texas tells Harris County to halt plan to send all voters applications for mail-in ballots.** Thankfully, Texas is one of few states standing up to ill-advised election day plans by some county leaders, in this case Harris County, which is trying to send all registered voters an application for an absentee ballot. While may seem like an added convenience for voters, the reality is that our voter rolls are terribly out of date, and such applications could go to bad addresses, to the deceased, or to the wrong person and then be abused by bad actors seeking to abuse our election process. Election security should always take precedent over convenience. Read more: [The Texas Tribune](https://truethevote.acemlnb.com/lt.php?s=8bd62f454a89069e58200a8ac4751d38&i=137A377A20A1419), 8/28/20

| | Friend - With the general election fast approaching, now is the time to make your voting plan. In a few short weeks, mail-in ballots will be going out to Georgia voters who request them. Are you planning to vote by mail? You can now request your mail-in ballot through Georgia's new online portal. [Click here to request your ballot online now](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4a8/34a064cc/1bce8dad/702dd4e/2285609023/VEsE/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://ballotrequest.sos.ga.gov/"). Requesting your ballot online is an easy and secure way to ensure you are prepared to vote in the most consequential election of our lives. Don't wait. Request your ballot as early as possible - TODAY! - to provide plenty of time for it to be mailed to you and returned by Election Day. Help us get the word out today by telling your friends and family they can request their ballots online at [https://ballotrequest.sos.ga.gov/](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4a8/34a064cc/1bce8dad/702dd4e/2285609023/VEsF/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://ballotrequest.sos.ga.gov/"). Want to make returning your ballot quick and easy? Consider placing it in a drop box! Contact your county election office to find a convenient drop box in your community. You can find your county election office's contact information [here](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4a8/34a064cc/1bce8dad/702dd4f/2285609023/VEsC/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "https://elections.sos.ga.gov/Elections/countyelectionoffices.do"). If you have any general questions about voting, you can go to [peachvote.com](https://secure.fairfightaction.com/page/m/41efa4a8/34a064cc/1bce8dad/702dd4c/2285609023/VEsD/?g=yzW-IFH8x8VbBlZb5iRRXGA "http://peachvote.com"). Now is the time to make our voices heard and votes counted, so let's get it done. In solidarity, Team Fair Fight Action |
